jokes.text: # Oh, Christmas tree, oh, Christmas tree, # You're on the curb and dead now # Your Christmas lights are in the box. In two months we will change the clocks #
jokes.punchline: Lyrics about post-Christmas disposal and mundane life
jokes.explanation: Clever parody that subverts familiar melody with post-holiday reality. Well-structured with good economy.

jokes.text: Gee, they sure give the Christmas spirit the bum's rush around here.
jokes.punchline: Homer's folksy observation about quick transition
jokes.explanation: Mild observational humor in Homer's voice but not particularly memorable or clever.

jokes.text: MEET MARTIN LUTHER KING - So, young man, have you judged people by the content of their character this year? - Yes, Reverend Doctor. - Lunchtime! - Free at last! Free at last!
jokes.punchline: Actor immediately abandoning dignity for lunch break
jokes.explanation: Sharp satirical timing, using MLK's famous words for mundane purpose. Well-crafted juxtaposition.

jokes.text: I'm returning this kitten calendar. - Um, I'm also returning this kitten calendar. - Kitten calendar.
jokes.punchline: Everyone got the same terrible gift
jokes.explanation: Solid family dysfunction joke with good timing, but fairly predictable premise.

jokes.text: Hey, those are 15 month calendars! That gives you three extra kittens!
jokes.punchline: Lisa's earnest attempt to find silver lining
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment - optimistic and logical in absurd situation.

jokes.text: Man, those are ugly kittens!
jokes.explanation: Simple Bart-style blunt observation, economical but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: It's so sterile!
jokes.punchline: Lisa's impressed reaction to clinical design
jokes.explanation: Spot-on Apple store satire, Lisa finding sterility appealing fits her character.

jokes.text: Operating System 4.2 has sloppier architecture than a Tijuana anthill. - Did you get peanut butter in your Ethernet port again? - No. I got mayonnaise in the CD drive.
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy pompous complaint escalating to absurd food mishaps. Great character integration.

jokes.text: It's fueled by dreams and powered by imagination. - What does it do? - You should ask yourself, 'What can I do for it?'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant satire of Apple's pretentious marketing speak. The reversal is perfectly crafted.

jokes.text: But it's glowing. - That light confirms that it's off.
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Apple's counterintuitive design philosophy satire.

jokes.text: I can't afford any of your products but can I buy some fake white earbuds, so people will think I have a MyPod? - Sure, those are called 'MyPhonies.' - Oh, and they cost $40.
jokes.setup: Milhouse wants to fake having Apple product
jokes.punchline: Product name pun and expensive fake accessories
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay and social satire about status symbols. Perfect Milhouse desperation.

jokes.text: I hate this MyPod! I can't watch movies on a screen this small. And the music today-- don't get me started!
jokes.punchline: Old man rant about modern technology
jokes.explanation: Standard old-person-complaining-about-technology bit, fits Krusty but not particularly fresh.

jokes.text: I said, don't get me started!
jokes.explanation: Weak escalation that doesn't add much to the previous joke.

jokes.text: Come on, isn't someone going to get me started?!
jokes.punchline: Desperately wanting attention for his rant
jokes.explanation: Better payoff to the running gag, showing Krusty's desperate need for attention.

jokes.text: Uh, let's go, Teeny. Maybe somebody at Old Navy will get me started.
jokes.punchline: Going to lower-tier store for attention
jokes.explanation: Good callback that shows Krusty's declining status and desperate need for validation.

jokes.text: Thank the Jewish Clown Awards gift basket.
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd and specific to Krusty's character. Great throwaway line.

jokes.text: And that announcement is... You're all losers!
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection that subverts corporate presentation expectations with brutal honesty.

jokes.text: You think you're cool because you buy a $500 phone with a picture of a fruit on it?! Well, guess what? They cost eight bucks to make and I pee on every one!
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the consumer criticism, though the pee joke is somewhat juvenile.

jokes.text: I have made a fortune off you chumps and I've invested it all in Microsoft! Now my boyfriend, Bill Gates, and I kiss each other on a pile of your money!
jokes.explanation: Escalates the betrayal to absurd level with tech rivalry and unexpected romance.

jokes.text: Traitor! Your heart is blacker than your turtleneck!
jokes.explanation: Perfect Comic Book Guy dramatic response with specific Steve Jobs reference.

jokes.text: Stupid angry mob, chasing me because I shine a harsh light on modern society. Now I know how Dane Cook feels.
jokes.explanation: Good self-aware joke about persecution complex, timely Dane Cook reference.

jokes.text: Mmm, what's that exotic aroma? It smells like a hamburger cooked at a rug store.
jokes.explanation: Funny specific description that's both appetizing and off-putting, very Bart-like.

jokes.text: Whoa! All these years I've been petting lambs when I should've been shoving them in my mouth!
jokes.setup: Bart trying lamb for first time
jokes.punchline: Crude realization about meat vs animals
jokes.explanation: Classic Bart crude observation that's true to character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Jordan. That's on some map somewhere, right?
jokes.explanation: Typical Bart ignorance, fits character but is a fairly standard joke.

jokes.text: Bashir, you didn't tell me you had a sister.
jokes.punchline: Bart hitting on what's likely Bashir's mother
jokes.explanation: Standard misunderstanding joke about age/family relationships, not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Hey, I never refuse food from strangers.
jokes.punchline: Bart stating opposite of standard safety advice
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Bart's reckless nature and love of food.

jokes.text: Ah, that's not clever! And I have so many aspects you can mock! I'm poor, I'm dirty, I can't read or write, I think movies are real!
jokes.explanation: Perfect Willie moment - self-aware about his flaws and offering better insult material.

jokes.text: coleslaw, tuna, all chowders and gumbos. The only thing that's safe is the pork chops. - My religion says I can never eat pork.
jokes.punchline: Only safe food is forbidden to Bashir
jokes.explanation: Good ironic setup showing cultural/religious differences through food.

jokes.text: A different religion?! Do not tell anyone that because if the bullies around here find out that you're different...
jokes.punchline: Immediate concern about bullying for difference
jokes.explanation: Sets up the coming conflict but not particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: 'Bash here'? I love a kid that comes with directions.
jokes.punchline: Name interpreted as instruction for violence
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that's both funny and menacing, perfect for bully character.

jokes.text: I'm going to punch you extra hard 'cause I secretly think you're cute.
jokes.explanation: Subverts typical bully motivation with confused sexuality angle.

jokes.text: You're the reason I can't carry toothpaste on an airplane!
jokes.punchline: Blaming him for TSA liquid restrictions
jokes.explanation: Timely reference to post-9/11 security measures, mildly clever but not exceptional.

jokes.text: Jimbo, you're Christian, Dolph, you're Jewish, and, Kearney, your family's in that cult Moe started. - I had to join. My mom's doing the savior. - I mean, the one true Moe.
jokes.punchline: Kearney's family in Moe's cult with sexual innuendo
jokes.explanation: Good escalation to absurd religion, Kearney's correction adds to the awkwardness.

jokes.text: Help me, almighty Moe! - Can't. I'm not really a god. I was just acting crazy to get out of jury duty.
jokes.setup: Kearney calling to Moe for help
jokes.punchline: Moe's mundane motivation for fake religion
jokes.explanation: Perfect Moe moment - deflating religious pretension with lazy excuse. Great callback payoff.

jokes.text: Look at Lisa Simpson, over there by herself. Not talking to us, her best friends! Do we hate her? YES!
jokes.punchline: Immediate shift to hatred over minor slight
jokes.explanation: Captures fickle nature of elementary school friendships but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Check out 'Moon Dreams' from Miles Davis' 'Birth of the cool'.
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lisa character moment but not inherently funny, more character building.

jokes.text: DIEPOD SLAYLIST
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay combining iPod terminology with violent cartoon theme.

jokes.text: Marge, I installed all the low-energy bulbs. What have you done with the old ones? - Disposed of them in an environmentally friendly manner.
jokes.explanation: Good visual contradiction of Marge's claim, environmental hypocrisy joke.

jokes.text: Salaam alaikum! - Salaam alaikum! - Milhouse is looking good! He's got contacts, changed color, got a cool new catchphrase.
jokes.punchline: Homer thinking Bashir is transformed Milhouse
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer obliviousness - can't tell different people apart, thinks greeting is catchphrase.

jokes.text: Sir? That's the kind of respect you'd have to strangle out of an American kid.
jokes.punchline: Homer's violent comparison to American kids
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about parenting and cultural differences, typical Homer bluntness.

jokes.text: You know, Bart never told me he had a handsome teenage brother. - Oh, uh, I'm really 38. - 38 waist I might believe.
jokes.explanation: Standard fat joke, Homer's obliviousness is character-appropriate but joke is weak.

jokes.text: Hey, Bashir's great. If Derek Jeter married Mariah Carey-- wouldn't last but I bet they'd have a kid like him.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre celebrity couple comparison with relationship prediction
jokes.explanation: Typical Homer non-sequitur celebrity comparison, character-appropriate randomness.

jokes.text: Homer, this is serious. This Bashir kid is Muslim, and therefore up to something.
jokes.explanation: Sets up prejudice theme but not particularly funny on its own, more plot development.

jokes.text: Oh, I can't believe that till I see a fictional TV program espousing your point of view.
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-commentary on how TV shapes opinions, perfect Homer characterization of media dependency.

jokes.text: For the last time, Fayed, where did you hide the nerve gas?! - Under your Statue of Liberty's dress! - And she loved it!
jokes.explanation: Parodies action show interrogation with juvenile humor, serves plot purpose but weak joke.

jokes.text: That's pretty patriotic, but I got a better idea. Invite 'em over. A little dinner, a little dessert, and then you Jack Bauer 'em into giving you all their secrets.
jokes.setup: Discussion of how to handle Muslim family
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition of friendly dinner with spy tactics, timely 24 reference.

jokes.text: Ah, interesting. Why, just the other day, I was reading that science is used to make bombs.
jokes.explanation: Homer's paranoid logic connecting innocent information to terrorism, character-appropriate.

jokes.text: What's the matter? Don't like the taste of freedom?
jokes.punchline: Homer making political statement about dessert
jokes.explanation: Good absurd escalation of patriotism applied to cake, economical and quotable.

jokes.text: Don't fear them just because they have a different religion, a different culture, and their last name is Bin Laden.
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection - Lisa's tolerance speech undercut by major reveal. Great structure and timing.

jokes.text: Young man, you do not respect us by disrespecting your father. - I like the way you Italians think.
jokes.explanation: Homer's continued obliviousness about ethnicity, character-consistent but mild joke.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. It's just so fun and easy to judge people based on religion.
jokes.punchline: Honest admission about prejudice being enjoyable
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer honesty about prejudice - darkly funny and self-aware. Great character moment.

jokes.text: But we're the more powerful country for a few more years.
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about American decline and power dynamics, economical and cutting.

jokes.text: I am sorry I suspected you of being soulless murders of innocent children.
jokes.explanation: Homer's typically excessive and inappropriate apology, character-consistent escalation.

jokes.text: Dynamite? They are terrorists. Must leave quietly.
jokes.explanation: Sets up misunderstanding plot but not particularly funny on its own.

jokes.text: Marge! I was right! Everybody is whatever I think they are.
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Homer logic - confuses confirmation bias with reality. Perfect character philosophy statement.

jokes.text: Hi, Homer! - You rubbed? You must like me. You really, really like me.
jokes.punchline: Homer's needy interpretation of lamp rubbing
jokes.explanation: Homer's desperate need for affection applied to genie mechanics, character-appropriate.

jokes.text: Good morning, Ramadan! Come to Crazy Fayeddie's! Our prices are Hussein!
jokes.setup: Genie appearing in stereotypical Middle Eastern business
jokes.punchline: Names used as adjectives in sales pitch
jokes.explanation: Weak wordplay relying on Middle Eastern names as puns, somewhat offensive.

jokes.text: I got to prove the genie was right. - Off to work. - Sometimes I wish you would quit that awful business. - But I love blowing up buildings.
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection setup - innocent demolition work sounds terroristic out of context.

jokes.text: Safely and legally, in order to make room for new buildings. - Darling, I think you are working much too hard. - Yes, I am killing myself, but it is all for the profit, and after the explosion, I will be in a better place. - That corner office with the downtown view.
jokes.punchline: Multiple phrases sounding terroristic but referring to promotion
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of misunderstood phrases - each one sounds worse but is innocent. Well-crafted.

jokes.text: I want to apologize for being such a jerk at dinner, and I thought the best way to do that would be to come to your house and poke around.
jokes.punchline: Admitting ulterior motive for apology visit
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer honesty about his suspicious motives, self-aware and economical.

jokes.text: I read somewhere your people are hospitable to guests. Is that true? - Yes. - Praise be Oliver. - That's Allah. - Yeah... we'll look it up in the Corona.
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of Homer's religious ignorance, each mistake worse than the last.

jokes.text: Ooh, here comes the ambulance. - My, you're a handsome fellow. - Just get in the ambulance, Mr. Simpson. We'll see to your finger.
jokes.explanation: Mild joke about Homer's inappropriate flirting, nothing exceptional.

jokes.text: Boy, don't blow up this mall. It has the cookie store that gives free samples.
jokes.setup: Homer warning Bart about mall bombing
jokes.punchline: Prioritizing free cookies over human life
jokes.explanation: Perfect Homer priorities - free food over everything else, character-consistent selfishness.

jokes.text: Did you factor in one bald idiot?
jokes.explanation: Good setup for Homer causing problems, economical description of Homer.

jokes.text: Did we really need a bridge there? - The Duff brewery is on that island.
jokes.explanation: Good Springfield-specific joke about beer priorities, local humor.

jokes.text: PARDON MY INTOLERANCE - That banner has really paid for itself over the years.
jokes.punchline: Homer's casual admission of repeated prejudice
jokes.explanation: Brilliant visual gag implying Homer's regular intolerance, perfect dark character moment.

jokes.text: She said something about 'the bottom of the ocean' and 'be back in a month.'
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to impossible underwater journey
jokes.explanation: Sets up Lisa's underwater adventure, absurd but serves plot function.

jokes.text: The cost of this journey will be added to your bill.
jokes.explanation: Good continuation of Apple's nickel-and-dime pricing satire.

jokes.text: I'm sorry. I know our posters say 'Think differently,' but our real slogan is 'No refunds.'
jokes.explanation: Sharp satire contrasting Apple's innovative image with corporate greed, well-crafted.
